case_title: LORENZO LOGORECI VS. ONE RINCON HILL ASSOCIATION ET AL

docket_entries.description: ARBITRATOR ROGER LOUIS MEREDITH APPOINTED. ARBITRATOR'S JURISDICTION AUG-28-2020 THROUGH NOV-26-2020.
ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR MAY-11-2021 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE OF COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100135470)
PANEL OF ARBITRATORS ASSIGNED. REJECTION DATE AUG-27-2020.

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAR-30-2021 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF LORENZO LOGORECI'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR ADMISSION, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO CORRECTION AND REFILING. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT JAMESHFLEMING@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: JAMES FLEMING; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Moton To Compel Response To Form Interrrogatories And Request For Admission (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Mar-30-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Moton To Compel Response To Form Interrrogatories And Request For Admission
Off Calendar Mar-30-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF LORENZO LOGORECI's Motion To Compel Response To Form Interrogatories And Request For Admission.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Court finds that moving party has failed to file a Separate Statement in support of the motion in violation of California Rule of Court 3.1345; has failed to attach Exhibit 13 to the filed or courtesy copies of the Declaration of Nathan filed March 22, 2021; has improperly combined a request for admission with another form of discovery request in violation of Code of Civil Procedure sec. 2033.060(h).

The Court further finds that responding party has failed to investigate plaintiff's claims with reasonable dispatch and has relied upon that failure to avoid responding to substantive discovery requests; has failed to respond fully and completely to interrogatories in violation of Code of Civil Procedure secs. 2030.220, 2030.240, 2033.220 and 2033.230, particularly as to the grounds of denial of plaintiff's claim by defendant's insurer with whom defendant and its counsel claim a tripartite relationship; has stated invalid and non-specific objections in unlabeled prefaces and General Objections; has stated additional invalid objections and qualifications to discovery responses.

The motion is therefore denied without prejudice to correction and refiling. Sanctions are denied without prejudice because the circumstances make the imposition thereof unjust. However, sanctions may be considered in the future for additional violations of the Discovery Act and/or the Rules of Court.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
